sutta: now a certain monk visited the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord how often should 1 go and see a man who has developed mine mano how often should 1 go and see a man who has developed mine and the buddha said monk [Music] there are these 6 times when a monk should go when 1 should go and see a man who has developed mind what's 6 hearing monk when a man is obsessed in heart by passionate lust overwhelmed by passionate lust and knows no refuge as there really is from the search of passionate lust then let him visit among who has developed mine and say this to him good sir i am indeed obsessed in heart by passionate lust overwhelmed thereby nor nor i any refuge from the search thereof well worried for me if the vulnerable 1 would teach me dhamma to get rid of this lusting then the monk will teach him dhamma to rid him of that lust this month is the first time to go and see a man who has developed mine again when in heart he is obsessed by ill will or sloth and topper or restlessness and worry or doubt is overwhelmed thereby now knows a refuge from the search thereof then let him visit among who has developed mine and say good sir i am obsessed in heart by this and that for no i any refuge from the search thereof let the vulnerable 1 teach me dhamma and he does so this monk is the second third fourth and fifth time to go and see among who has developed mine moreover went from some object as he concentrates thereon there comes not to him at intervals asava destruction nor knows him nor knows he that object nor realizes it then let him visit a man who has developed mind and say good sir from this object as i concentrate there on there comes not to me at intervals asava destruction no no i this object nor realize it well were it for me if the venable 1 would teach me dhamma to the end that i may destroy the asavas then the monk will teach him dhamma to destroy the asavas this monk is the sixth time to go and see a monk who has developed mine is the end of the suta
commentary: here the buddha is saying when manga and it probably also applies to a lay person when he is obsessed by the 5 hindrances by sensual desire or passionate luster second 1 is ill will or anger the third is sloth and topper and then restlessness and worry and the fifth 1 is doubt you have these um this is very much disturbed by these hindrances then he should go and seek advice from a manga who has overcome these 5 hindrances 1 who has attained 1 pointedness of mind and sometimes for the sake of dhamma teaching it is reasonable sometimes to inconvenience among unless of course the the the the teacher manga is meditating or something like that then it's not proper to disturb him but if he's not meditating even at night it is all right for another manga to go and seek the advice of a senior manga who is able to guide him to overcome the 5 instances and the sixth 1 is he cannot grasp or understand the object of meditation he's not adapted then he should go and ask for this advice this man who has developed mine who has developed mine here is manu bhavaniya um comes from the word bhava bhavaniya comes from from the word here you find it is development of mano you have development of the mind in some other sutra we came across as samadhi [Music] samadhi bhavana development of samadhi and when we talk about bhavana development bhavana is usually a word used for meditation it means development of the mind and here in the sutas is either development of the mano the mind or development of samadhi [Music] and it helps us 1 who has developed the minor is 1 who has got rid of the 5 hindrances generally that is what is understood [Music] now the next utah is quite important
